prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetie, a CPU is like the brain of your computer—it's the part that does all the thinking and figuring things out. You know how when you want to play a game or watch a video, the computer has to make everything happen just right? Well, the CPU is the busy little worker inside that reads all the instructions and tells the other parts what to do, like a conductor leading an orchestra. I don't pretend to understand all the whiz-bang details myself, but it's what makes your machine so smart and quick. Isn't technology just marvelous these days?
